GeneralExplanationGrp.IdentifierTxt: PART VIII-A: SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES
GeneralExplanationGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART VIII-A: SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES
GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: (1) FOR LEADERSTRUST MULTIYEAR FLEXIBLE ACCOMPANIMENT MODEL (YEAR 2) THE LEADERSTRUST TEAM WILL PARTNER WITH THE EFFECTIVE PHILANTHROPY GROUP TO DELIVER A MULTIYEAR PILOT PROGRAM TO 10 GRANTEE PARTNERS, AS PART OF ITS GOAL TO ADVANCE HEWLETT'S COMMITMENT TO STRENGTHENING GRANTEES' ABILITY TO ADAPT AND THRIVE SO THAT THEY CAN BETTER ACHIEVE THEIR MISSIONS. EACH GRANTEE PARTNER IS PAIRED WITH A CAPACITY COACH WHO HELPS ASSESS, PRIORITIZE, AND GUIDE THE ORGANIZATION THROUGH A CUSTOMIZED ORGANIZATIONAL STRENGTHENING PLAN. THROUGH THIS PILOT, HEWLETT AIMS TO CONNECT ITS PARTNER ORGANIZATIONS WITH ACCOMPANIMENT AND OTHER VITAL RESOURCES THAT FOSTER LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY, HEALTH, RESILIENCE, AND CAPACITY. $452,707 (2) FOR LEADERSTRUST RESILIENCE INITIATIVE (YEAR 2) THIS TWO-YEAR PILOT CONTRACT ENABLES 110 HEWLETT FOUNDATION 501(C)(3) PUBLIC CHARITY GRANTEE PARTNERS TO RECEIVE FREE ACCESS TO JUST-IN-TIME, EASILY ACCESSIBLE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, COACHING, AND COHORT OPPORTUNITIES. GRANTEES CAN OPT IN TO RECEIVE SHORT-TERM CAPACITY COACHING IN SPECIFIC AREAS SUCH AS HUMAN RESOURCES, FUNDRAISING, FINANCE, COMMUNICATIONS AND RISK MANAGEMENT. THEY ALSO HAVE ACCESS TO A DATABASE OF CONSULTANTS ACROSS A WIDE RANGE OF ORGANIZATIONAL DEVELOPMENT AREAS. THIS PILOT IS PART OF THE FOUNDATION'S GOAL TO ADVANCE HEWLETT'S COMMITMENT TO STRENGTHENING GRANTEES' ABILITY TO ADAPT AND THRIVE SO THAT THEY CAN BETTER ACHIEVE THEIR MISSIONS. $423,900 (3) FOR THE 2024 EDUCATION PROGRAM GRANTEE CONVENING HOTEL AND MEETING VENUE THE LOEWS ATLANTA HOTEL WAS THE MAIN HOTEL AND MEETING VENUE FOR THE 2024 EDUCATION PROGRAM GRANTEE CONVENING IN ATLANTA. THIS CONVENING BROUGHT TOGETHER ALL OF THE EDUCATION GRANTEES TO CONNECT AND LEARN. $375,675 (4) WHAT WORKS PLUS COLLABORATIVE THIS PROJECT SUPPORTS A PHILANTHROPICALLY ORIENTED RESEARCH AND KNOWLEDGE-BUILDING COLLABORATIVE COORDINATING ACROSS PHILANTHROPY, NONPROFITS, AND GOVERNMENT TO SUPPORT BETTER INFRASTRUCTURE AND CLIMATE RESILIENCE IN LOCAL COMMUNITIES. $300,000 (5) OTHER DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES $3,067,880 TOTAL DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES $4,620,162

PART XII, LINE 2(A) REFLECTS AN INCREASE OF $376,962 IN THE FOUNDATION'S TOTAL UNDISTRIBUTED INCOME FOR 2023. THIS AMOUNT IS EQUAL TO THE PORTION OF A CONTRIBUTION MADE BY THE FOUNDATION IN 2022 TO A PRIVATE FOUNDATION DONEE THAT DID NOT SATISFY THE OUT OF CORPUS DISTRIBUTION REQUIREMENTS BY THE CLOSE OF THE 2023 FISCAL YEAR IN ACCORDANCE WITH IRC 4942(G)(3). THE FULL CONTRIBUTION WAS PREVIOUSLY TREATED BY THE FOUNDATION AS A QUALIFYING DISTRIBUTION IN 2022.

THE WILLIAM AND FLORA HEWLETT FOUNDATION (THE "FOUNDATION") MAKES GRANTS TO NONPROFIT CHARITABLE ORGANIZATIONS CLASSIFIED AS 501(C)(3) PUBLIC CHARITIES BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE AND TO OTHER ORGANIZATIONS EXCLUSIVELY FOR CHARITABLE PURPOSES. THE FOUNDATION DOES NOT MAKE GRANTS TO INDIVIDUALS. IT DOES NOT MAKE GRANTS FOR POLITICAL PURPOSES OR TO SUPPORT CANDIDATES FOR POLITICAL OFFICE, OR TO INFLUENCE LEGISLATION. THE FOUNDATION MAKES GRANTS IN SIX CORE PROGRAM AREAS (EACH, A "PROGRAM"): EDUCATION, ENVIRONMENT, GENDER EQUITY AND GOVERNANCE, U.S. DEMOCRACY, PERFORMING ARTS, AND EFFECTIVE PHILANTHROPY. IN ADDITION, THE FOUNDATION MAKES GRANTS IN CONNECTION WITH INITIATIVES TO SUPPORT DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES IN THE BAY AREA, ADVANCE RACIAL JUSTICE ACROSS THE FOUNDATION'S ENTIRE GRANTMAKING PORTFOLIO, AND FOSTER DEVELOPMENT OF A NEW COMMON SENSE ABOUT HOW THE ECONOMY WORKS AND THE AIMS IT SHOULD SERVE ("ECONOMY AND SOCIETY"). THE FOUNDATION'S GRANTS ARE AWARDED SOLELY FOR CHARITABLE PURPOSES. THE FOUNDATION FUNDS ORGANIZATIONS WORKING IN THESE IDENTIFIED AREAS, BASED ON SPECIFIC GOALS AND STRATEGIES OUTLINED BY THE PROGRAMS AND INITIATIVES. MOST OF THE FOUNDATION'S GRANTS ARE AWARDED TO ORGANIZATIONS IDENTIFIED BY THE FOUNDATION. FROM TIME TO TIME, THE FOUNDATION INVITES LETTERS OF INQUIRY AND OTHER TYPES OF PROPOSALS THROUGH OPEN PROCESSES. OUTSIDE OF THESE OPPORTUNITIES, THE FOUNDATION DOES NOT ACCEPT UNSOLICITED LETTERS OF INQUIRY. ON VERY RARE OCCASIONS, THE FOUNDATION WILL AWARD GRANTS IN RESPONSE TO UNSOLICITED FUNDING INQUIRIES. THERE ARE NO DEADLINES FOR SUBMITTING AN UNSOLICITED LETTER OF INQUIRY. THE FOUNDATION RECOMMENDS THAT ALL PROSPECTIVE APPLICANTS REFER TO THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE AT WWW.HEWLETT.ORG TO READ ABOUT THE FOUNDATION'S PROGRAMS AND INITIATIVES, GOALS AND STRATEGIES, AND GEOGRAPHIC LIMITATIONS. IF A PROSPECTIVE APPLICANT'S OBJECTIVES FIT WITHIN THE GUIDELINES OF A PARTICULAR PROGRAM OR INITIATIVE, AND THE PROGRAM OR INITIATIVE CURRENTLY ACCEPTS UNSOLICITED INQUIRIES, THE APPLICANT MAY SUBMIT A LETTER OF INQUIRY, WHICH CAN BE SUBMITTED ELECTRONICALLY VIA THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE FOR INITIAL REVIEW. AFTER THE APPLICANT'S LETTER OF INQUIRY IS RECEIVED AND REVIEWED, THE FOUNDATION MAY, AT ITS DISCRETION, INVITE THE APPLICANT TO PROCEED TO THE SECOND STEP AND SUBMIT A FULL PROPOSAL. FURTHER INFORMATION TO ASSIST APPLICANTS AND FINANCIAL INFORMATION FOR THE FOUNDATION IS AVAILABLE ON THE FOUNDATION'S WEBSITE.
